description: Alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency (SERPINA1) risk-genotype panel: Pi*Z (rs28929474) and Pi*S (rs17580). Genotype-combination calls (PiZZ/PiSZ/PiSS/PiMZ/PiMS). Actionable (smoking avoidance, augmentation eligibility, liver monitoring). EXPANSION_STRATEGY.md §6 / roadmap #25.

genotype_models.finding_text: Alpha-1 antitrypsin PiZZ ({genotype}) — the severe-deficiency genotype (serum AAT typically <30% of normal), strongly associated with early-onset emphysema/COPD and with liver disease. This is actionable: smoking and vaping avoidance is the single most important step, augmentation therapy may be an option, and periodic liver monitoring is advised. AATD is substantially under-diagnosed, so a confirmed PiZZ result is worth acting on.

Alpha-1 antitrypsin PiSZ ({genotype}) — intermediate deficiency with increased risk of lung (and some liver) disease, especially with smoking. Smoking/vaping avoidance is the highest-leverage step; discuss serum AAT testing and monitoring with a clinician.
Alpha-1 antitrypsin PiSS ({genotype}) — mildly reduced AAT levels; clinical risk is low, particularly in non-smokers. Avoiding smoking remains advisable.

Alpha-1 antitrypsin PiMZ ({genotype}) — you carry one Z allele. AAT levels are mildly reduced; risk is low overall but can rise with smoking. Mainly relevant for family planning and as a reason to avoid smoking.
Alpha-1 antitrypsin PiMS ({genotype}) — you carry one S allele. This is of minimal clinical significance on its own; mainly relevant for family planning.
